data.title: Will Trump Greenlight Anthropic's Mythos After The Pentagon Fight?
data.description:
Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark said the firm is discussing its next frontier model with the Trump administration despite a Pentagon contract dispute that led the agency to label the company as a supply chain risk.

"AI models have reached a level of coding capability where they can surpass all but the most skilled humans at finding and exploiting software vulnerabilities," Anthropic noted. Mythos Preview is an AI model that exposes software vulnerabilities, helping companies protect themselves from threats.

The development handed the Trump administration a key interim victory in a closely watched legal fight over artificial intelligence and military use.

The ruling allows the designation to remain in effect while litigation continues, though it does not resolve the case on its merits.

“We have a narrow contracting dispute, but I don’t want that to get in the way of the fact that we care deeply about national security,” Clark said at the Semafor World Economy event in Washington on Monday.

“Our position is the government has to know about this stuff, and we have to find new ways for the government to partner with a private sector that is making things that are truly revolutionizing the economy, but are going to have aspects to them which hit national security, equities, and other ones. So absolutely, we’re talking to them about Mythos, and we’ll talk to them about the next models as well," he continued.

Specific details regarding the discussions with the U.S. government—including which agencies are involved—have not been disclosed, according to Reuters.

Photo: Shutterstock

data.title: Nvidia Shatters The GPU-Only Myth With New Multi-Chip AI Strategy
data.description:
Shift Toward Task-Specific Chips

Faster Output For Large AI Models

Counterpoint says Nvidia is targeting demanding AI use cases, especially large models with over 100 billion parameters, where speed and response quality matter most. By separating tasks across GPUs and LPUs, the system improves the speed at which it generates outputs, particularly for long, multi-step interactions.

Long-Term Push To Lower Costs And Expand

Nvidia plans to start with high-end AI workloads and gradually expand into mid-range markets as costs decline. Counterpoint notes that this phased strategy allows Nvidia to first focus on premium applications before scaling adoption more broadly over time.

Technical Analysis

Nvidia is holding in the upper half of its 52-week range, sitting below the $212.19 high but well above the $95.04 low, which suggests buyers still control the bigger picture. The stock is trading 8.7% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 5.7% above its 100-day SMA, a setup that points to strength in both the short- and intermediate-term trends.

The moving average convergence divergence (MACD), a trend/momentum indicator, is bullish with the MACD line above the signal line and a positive histogram, consistent with upside momentum remaining in place. When MACD stays above its signal line, it usually means pullbacks are getting bought faster than they're sold.

Over the last 12 months, the stock has risen 74.46%, which aligns with a longer-term uptrend that has rewarded staying with the trend. The golden cross that formed in June 2025 (50-day SMA above the 200-day SMA) still matters because it often acts as a "trend permission slip" for longer-duration buyers.

  • Key Resistance: $197.50 — a nearby ceiling where rallies have recently stalled.
  • Key Support: $164.50 — an area where buyers have tended to step in.

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Looking further out, the next major catalyst for the stock arrives with the May 27, 2026 (estimated) earnings report.

  • EPS Estimate: $1.75 (Up from 96 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $78.72 Billion (Up from $44.06 Billion YoY)
  • Valuation: P/E of 38.6x (Indicates premium valuation relative to peers)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $281.04. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Benchmark: Buy (Maintains Target to $250.0000) (March 31)
  • Rosenblatt: Buy (Maintains Target to $325.00) (March 23)
  • Cantor Fitzgerald: Overweight (Maintains Target to $300.00) (March 23)

Top ETF Exposure

Significance: Because NVDA carries such a heavy weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows will likely trigger automatic buying or selling of the stock.

Price Action

NVDA Stock Price Activity: Nvidia shares were up 1.81% at $192.73 at the time of publication on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: Seanergy Maritime CEO On Positioning For Capesize Upside
data.description:
Mr. Stamatis Tsantanis, Chairman and CEO, and Mr. Stavros Gyftakis CFO, discussed the company's evolution into a pure-play Capesize platform, its hybrid chartering approach, and its disciplined capital allocation strategy, while outlining expectations for supply-demand dynamics in the Capesize segment.

You can watch the full discussion here:

A Pure Play Capesize Company Focusing on the Large Dry Bulk Segment

Since its 2015 relaunch, Seanergy has grown from a single vessel into a 20-vessel fleet focused exclusively on large dry bulk segments. The fleet consists of 18 Capesize ships and 2 Newcastlemax vessels. Total carrying capacity stands at 3.6 million dwt, with plans to expand to approximately 4.2 million dwt through newbuild deliveries.

As pointed out during the presentation, the company has invested over $425 million to build one of the industry's lowest book values per deadweight, reflecting a disciplined, counter-cyclical acquisition strategy.

Over this period, the company has combined fleet expansion with capital returns, distributing $51.2 million in dividends since 2022, or approximately $2.64 per share.

Fleet Modernization & Commercial Strategy

Fleet renewal remains a core pillar of Seanergy's strategy. The company is currently executing a $380 million fleet renewal program.

A key differentiator for the company is its commercial strategy. Seanergy maintains 100% time charter coverage across its fleet, but with contracts structured on an index-linked basis. This allows the company to capture spot market upside while maintaining contracted employment.

Currently, approximately 50% of the fleet is fixed at around $29,300 per day, demonstrating the company's ability to actively manage market conditions and secure profitable coverage.

Capital Allocation & Shareholder Returns

Seanergy has built a clearly defined capital allocation framework with a strong emphasis on returning cash to shareholders.

Since late 2021, the company has delivered close to $100 million in total shareholder returns.

Management outlined a structured financial approach, combining disciplined deleveraging with efficient capital deployment. The company targets a net loan-to-value ratio of approximately 50%.

The company's dividend policy follows a clear formula: 50% of free cash flow is distributed to shareholders, while the remaining 50% is retained to support capital expenditures and balance sheet strength.

Market Outlook and Geopolitical Impact

Seanergy sees a constructive setup for the Capesize market, supported by tightening supply and resilient demand trends.

On the demand side, strong Brazilian iron ore exports and continued growth in bauxite volumes are expected to support seaborne trade. Coal demand could increase as energy markets adjust, particularly in response to natural gas constraints.

Management indicated that this shift could add 100–200 million tons of incremental coal demand, providing a meaningful boost to dry bulk trade.

On the supply side, fundamentals appear particularly favorable. The Capesize and Newcastlemax segments currently have the lowest order book in approximately 20 years, while a significant portion of the global fleet is aging, with around 40% expected to exceed 15 years of age by 2026.

At the same time, shipyard capacity remains constrained, limiting the ability to deliver new vessels in the near term.

image credit: Author

Benzinga Disclaimer: This article is from an unpaid external contributor. It does not represent Benzinga's reporting and has not been edited for content or accuracy.

data.title: Why Is Ford Motor Stock Gaining Today?
data.description:
Optimism grew around the company's earnings outlook, supported by expectations of stronger execution and margin expansion.

UBS analyst Joseph Spak upgrades the stock from Neutral to Buy and maintains the price forecast of $15, citing a clear earnings growth trajectory.

The analyst said the firm sees a credible path for Ford to generate more than $2 earnings per share by 2027. He noted UBS estimates EPS at $2.08, roughly 17% above broader market consensus expectations.

Beyond 2027, he expects Ford to progress toward $3 in EPS driven by product and strategy shifts.

Spak added that a lenient U.S. regulatory backdrop and pragmatic EV approach support long-term profitability expansion.

He also highlighted opportunities in battery energy storage systems and higher-margin Pro software initiatives.

The analyst said shares faced pressure due to concerns over gasoline and aluminum prices in recent months.

However, Spak believes those concerns are overstated, noting aluminum costs are hedged and unlikely to impact 2026. He said the market is pricing in 2027 EPS of $1.73, about 16% below UBS forecasts.

He added investors are underestimating how temporary headwinds could fade in late 2026 and beyond.

In a separate note, Goldman Sachs analyst Mark Delaney maintains a Neutral rating on the stock, lowering the price forecast from $15 to $13.

F price Action: Ford Motor shares are trading higher by 3.62% to $12.60 at last check Tuesday.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: Natera Positioned To Capture Growing $20 Billion MRD Opportunity-Analyst
data.description:
MRD refers to the small number of cancer cells remaining in a patient's body after treatment, even when in remission and with no symptoms.

MRD testing is highly sensitive and is crucial for monitoring blood cancers (leukemia, lymphoma, myeloma) to predict relapse, guide treatment adjustments, and determine prognosis.

Market Opportunity And Growth Outlook

Analyst Andrew Brackmann said the MRD testing market, estimated at more than $20 billion, is still in early stages, with penetration currently in the mid- to high-single digits.

While multiple players are expected to gain traction, he emphasized that Natera is particularly well-positioned to benefit from increasing adoption in solid tumors.

Brackmann estimates Natera generated close to $1 billion in MRD revenue in 2025 and expects this segment to grow more than 40% in 2026.

He added that MRD revenue could expand more than fivefold over time as clinical evidence strengthens, guidelines evolve, and reimbursement improves.

Natera Competitive Moat Across Key Segments

The analyst pointed to Natera's sustained investments in clinical evidence, commercial infrastructure, and operational scale as key drivers of its competitive advantage.

In noninvasive prenatal testing (NIPT), Natera entered an already established market but has since captured more than 50% market share through its Panorama and Horizon offerings.

Similarly, in MRD, the company's early-mover advantage in clinical data, reimbursement access, and intellectual property has positioned it strongly against competitors.

Natera Diversified Growth And Long-Term Positioning

Beyond MRD and NIPT, analyst Brackmann wrote in an investor note on Monday that Natera's organ health segment has delivered outsized growth relative to peers, further reinforcing its multi-segment strategy.

While acknowledging that new entrants could gain traction and build physician awareness, the analyst wrote that Natera's growth trajectory and long-term market share are unlikely to face meaningful disruption.

He also emphasized that the company's pipeline provides multiple growth avenues, reducing reliance on any single product launch or indication. This diversified approach, he said, supports a clearer and more durable investment thesis over the coming years.

NTRA Stock Price Activity: Natera shares were up 4.44% at $210.88 at the time of publication on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image via Shutterstock
